results.title: 😸 5 million sales in 5 days | Product Hunt
results.content:
Since its early-access launch on Steam five days ago, it has sold over 5 million copies, and it’s gotten the gaming world's attention.

But what is it? Palworld is a survival-adventure game developed by Japanese indie studio, Pocket Pair. The announcement trailer was originally released in 2021, and it starts off fairly cutesy. You control a character roaming a lush and vibrant world where you come across and befriend numerous little critters that can help you build your home, craft tools, and a farm for food.

Then you see the guns. Suddenly, the trailer shifts gear from a cute Animal Crossing / Pokémon vibe to a more FPS atmosphere you might see in some triple-A games. [...] That’s where the survival aspect comes in. While the world looks lush, it’s pretty inhospitable, and you have to survive by building shelter, finding food, and fighting enemies – with guns. You can even give your pals (the monsters are called pals) their own guns to fight alongside you.

The controversy: A number of people have noticed some striking resemblance to a certain other Japanese monster-hunting game, but of course, your favorite little pika is bearing arms. According to Kotaku, the developers aren’t too concerned, insisting that the game passed legal reviews and isn’t infringing on Pokémon’s copyright.

Will you play it?

## Your canvas just learned to design

Newsletter Sponsor

newsletter icon

Monday through Friday

# 5 million sales in 5 days

Happy Hump Day! In case you missed it: The 2023 Golden Kitty Award winners have been announced. Here’s how one maker reacted to winning the Developer Tools award.

Now for some news:

🚗 Tesla is working on a new crossover car – with a more budget-friendly price.

🍎 Apple is reportedly building an EV, but its launch has been pushed back.

🎞️ Netflix is axing its cheapest ad-free tier in favor of a pricier option.

## This game sold over 5 million copies in five days

We’re barely a month into 2024, and we might already have the biggest game of the year. No, Spider-Man 3 did not drop overnight. It’s a survival-adventure game that’s being described as “Pokémon with guns.”

results.title: HakkoAI: Your best friend and advisor in PC games - Product Hunt
results.content:
It would be awesome to tell you how to build the best characters and how to approach building teams in games according to different constraints (like how the day echelons were very different from night echelons in GFL 1)

At the moment it seems like that's where it's headed and I'd love to have a companion in Palworld also to help me breed new species of pals without having to manually search the details outside of the game.

YUKI KE

This solves such a real problem — alt-tabbing mid-game is the worst. Love that it overlays directly on the game screen with real-time screen recognition.The 30 min daily free time is super generous too!

Quick question: which games does it work best with currently?

Definitely checking this out. Best of luck! 🚀🎮

Report

Mia

HakkoAI

Maker [...] ### HakkoAI Outperforms Most VLMs in Gaming Scenarios

HakkoAI is powered by LynkSoul VLM v1 a visual language model developed by our team and trained on massive amounts of gaming data. This allows the AI to truly see and understand gameplay scenarios with exceptional visual awareness. Thanks to its highly specialized training, LynkSoul VLM v1 stands as the most effective visual language model built for gaming.

results.title: Ghost: Open-source, self-hosted game servers | Product Hunt
results.content:
Hayden Bleasel
Ultracite

Ultracite

@ebazan33 You're welcome to check! I timed it spinning up a few servers - Terraria, CS, etc. TL;DR there's a pre-built image that you create when you add your Hetzner token. That has the basics covered. Then when you pick a game, it just restores that snapshot and downloads a docker image, more or less.

Elian Bazan

@haydenbleasel Snapshot handles the slow stuff once, then game-switching is just docker pull + start. That's why it can be fast. Most game-server tools re-provision from scratch every time and wonder why it takes 5 minutes.

Justin Seyvecou
PanicMode

PanicMode

Will it only support Hetzner Cloud or do you plan to expand in the future with other providers like OVH Cloud?

Hayden Bleasel
Ultracite

Ultracite [...] Hayden Bleasel
Ultracite

Ultracite

Marvin Danig

Hayden Bleasel
Ultracite

Ultracite

@achiral Not at all. Goal is eventually to make the backend swappable 👍

Marvin Danig

@haydenbleasel perfect! starred.

Elian Bazan

The "in seconds" claim is the part most game-server tools struggle with. What's actually happening in the background when a user spins up a Minecraft server? Pre-built images?
